claims.text: Softball practice has been pushed to next week.

claims.speaker: Michael Sei

claims.subject: Michael Sei
claims.speaker: Michael Sei
claims.evidence: No no no, I was joking with Alex.

claims.text: Layne Nicohl reacted negatively (thumbs down) to an earlier message in this thread.
claims.subject: Layne Nicohl
claims.speaker: Layne Nicohl
claims.evidence: thumbs_down [reaction]

claims.text: Chris Weihs went through multiple drafts with Stephen Andrews on an unspecified topic and was being detail-oriented about the process
claims.subject: Chris Weihs
claims.speaker: Chris Weihs
claims.evidence: I was being picky

claims.text: Chris Weihs is comfortable and non-judgmental with Stephen Andrews about iteration and revision cycles
claims.subject: Chris Weihs
claims.speaker: Chris Weihs
claims.evidence: All good

claims.text: Stephen Andrews believes most people hire wedding planners because they don't want to plan, not to enhance existing planning.
claims.subject: Stephen Andrews
claims.speaker: Stephen Andrews
claims.evidence: I think most people hire wedding planners because they DONT want to plan

claims.text: Stephen Andrews characterized Catherine Cole's approach to wedding planning as using a planner to enhance her own planning rather than delegate it.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Stephen Andrews
claims.evidence: You just want to enhance your planning with a planner steroid

claims.text: Catherine Cole is using AI image generation to iterate on wedding table designs.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: I wrangled AI to produce something simpler with more candles, all at the same level, and wood chairs.

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole prefers fewer candles in the wedding table design, finding the high-density candle arrangement crowded and aesthetically unbalanced.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I think the first photo, with fewer candles, looks better - the other one looks too crowded and a little…odd maybe?

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole believes that an excess of candles without flowers looks unbalanced and gives the impression of skimping on florals.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: It almost looks like we were trying to save money by not having any flowers, and then got carried away with candles.

claims.text: Catherine Cole's wedding table aesthetic vision is 'more-is-more' with candles — high density, bold, and impactful.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: The vision is more-is-more candles

claims.text: Catherine Cole found the original, airier candle arrangement with different heights too generic and insufficiently impactful.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: It felt generic. Less impactful

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole believes good design benefits from white space and breathing room, applying this principle to the candle arrangement debate.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: it's like with music and design generally - it helps to have white space (or silence) to allow it to breathe

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole strongly dislikes 'bread face' wedding aesthetics and considers them ghoulish and inappropriate for a wedding.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I loathe bread faces for a wedding. I feel quite strongly about it

claims.text: Catherine Cole liked the 'bread face' wedding aesthetic, putting her at odds with Mary Jo Cole on that reference point.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: I loved the bread-face wedding; you did not.

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole likes the mirrors under the plates and the draped plain white tablecloths in the wedding table design.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I like the mirrors under the plates… I like the draping of the tablecloths. And I think they're plain white, which I like.

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole prefers adding more flowers to the arrangement and believes it improves the design.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I do like that better, with more flowers.

claims.text: Catherine Cole acknowledged she partially disagrees with Mary Jo Cole's feedback on the candle density but recognized she may be outnumbered.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: I kinda disagree but I may be outnumbered here

claims.text: Catherine Cole committed to sleeping on the wedding table design decision before making a final call.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: Okay I will sleep on this

claims.text: Catherine Cole and Mary Jo Cole have an established pattern of aesthetic disagreement, referencing a prior 'bread face wedding' as a known point of divergence.
claims.subject: Catherine Cole
claims.speaker: Catherine Cole
claims.evidence: I fear this is a bread-face situation. I loved the bread-face wedding; you did not.

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole noted that the original airy candle arrangement may have looked less appealing partly because it appeared fall-like rather than summery, suggesting the wedding is in summer.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: it may be because it looks way more fall-like than summery

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole gave Stephen Andrews a birthday gift that included a card and money.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Stephen Andrews
claims.evidence: Thank you so much for the birthday gift! I loved the card so much—it's still sitting on my desk. And thank you for the money!

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole offered to Venmo Stephen Andrews $175 for insurance.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: I can Venmo you $175 for the insurance. Just tell me what your name is on Venmo so it goes to the right person.

claims.text: Stephen Andrews needs to provide his Venmo username to Mary Jo Cole so she can send the $175 insurance payment.
claims.subject: Stephen Andrews
claims.speaker: Mary Jo Cole
claims.evidence: Just tell me what your name is on Venmo so it goes to the right person.

claims.text: Mary Jo Cole and Stephen Andrews have a warm relationship; she gives him birthday gifts and is reimbursing him for insurance costs.
claims.subject: Mary Jo Cole
claims.speaker: Stephen Andrews
claims.evidence: Thank you so much for the birthday gift! I loved the card so much—it's still sitting on my desk.

claims.text: GUARD bill will not be added to the March 19 Senate Judiciary executive business meeting.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: No, it won't be added to next week's.

claims.text: GUARD bill markup is likely delayed until April.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: There's an outside chance it could get listed on Monday to be held over, but would probably be the following week

claims.text: Will Simpson will update Grassley with a whip count after speaking with Schmitt and Cornyn, and attempt to convince Grassley to list the bill without waiting for White House feedback.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: Once we talk to Schmitt and Cornyn I'll go back to Grassley with a whip count update and see if we can talk then into listing without WH feedback

claims.text: The White House Office of Legislative Affairs has been telling Grassley to wait one more week for their position on the GUARD bill, dragging it out for weeks.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: WH OLA keeps telling Grassley to just wait one more week and they will let them know their position. Grassley's fear is WH opposition the day before a

claims.text: Grassley's concern is that White House opposition announced the day before a markup would blow it up with drama.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: Grassley's fear is WH opposition the day before a markup that just blows it up with drama

claims.text: Vijay Menon believes the team will want Rule V to apply to the GUARD bill.

claims.speaker: Vijay Menon
claims.evidence: we will want to have Rule V apply to our bill

claims.text: Vijay Menon believes the White House is weighing how aggressively to oppose the GUARD bill.

claims.speaker: Vijay Menon
claims.evidence: I'm sure the WH is weighing how berserk they want to go here

claims.text: Senator Schmitt is currently leaning no on the chatbot bill and is very skeptical of AI regulations in general.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: Schmitt is still leaning no on the chatbot bill right now, very skeptical of AI regs in general

claims.text: Ethan has a couple of amendment ideas for the chatbot bill that he thinks might bring Schmitt on board, with one being a harder ask.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: Ethan has a couple of ideas for amendments that he thinks might help. I think they are mostly doable, one is a harder ask

claims.text: Senator Schmitt will not draft amendments himself — he is only sharing amendment ideas conceptually.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: He is not going to draft them, just giving them conceptually

claims.text: Will Simpson is uncertain how much the team should edit the manager's amendment without a promise of Schmitt's support in return.
claims.subject: Will Simpson
claims.speaker: Will Simpson
claims.evidence: I'm not sure how much we want to further edit our manager's amendment without a promise of his support
